subject: history
description: IB DP History: prescribed subjects (source-based), world history topics (20th century), HL regional options. Focus on historiography and analysis.

domains.name: 20世纪世界史 / 20th Century World History / 20th Century World History

nodes.name: 第一次世界大战
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the long-term and short-term causes of the First World War, evaluating the relative significance of factors such as militarism, alliances, imperialism, and nationalism (HL/SL).
Students will be able to assess the social, economic, and political consequences of WWI, including the Treaty of Versailles and its impact on the post-war order.

nodes.name: 俄国革命
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the causes of the 1917 February and October Revolutions, evaluating the roles of the Tsarist regime, WWI, and revolutionary leadership (HL/SL).
Students will be able to compare and contrast the nature of the Provisional Government and the Bolshevik state under Lenin.

nodes.name: 战间期
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the challenges to democracy and the rise of authoritarian states in the interwar period, using specific case studies (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the causes and consequences of the Great Depression on international relations and domestic politics.

nodes.name: 第二次世界大战
curriculum_points: Students will be able to assess the causes of WWII, analysing the role of appeasement, the failure of collective security, and the aggressive foreign policies of Germany, Italy, and Japan (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the social, economic, and technological impact of total war on civilian populations.

nodes.name: 冷战
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the origins of the Cold War, analysing ideological, political, and economic differences between the superpowers (HL/SL).
Students will be able to compare and contrast the nature and impact of Cold War crises (e.g., Berlin, Cuba) and conflicts in different regions.

nodes.name: 非殖民化
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the causes and processes of decolonization in two regions, evaluating the role of nationalist movements, colonial policies, and the Cold War (HL/SL).
Students will be able to assess the challenges faced by newly independent states in the post-colonial period.

domains.name: 权威与威权 / Authoritarian States / Authoritarian States

nodes.name: 希特勒统治下的德国
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate the methods used by the Nazi regime to establish and maintain total control, including the use of terror, propaganda, and economic policies (HL/SL).
Students will be able to analyse the ideological roots and implementation of Nazi racial and social policies, including the persecution of minorities and the Holocaust.

nodes.name: 斯大林的苏联
curriculum_points: Students will be able to assess the methods used by Stalin to consolidate power, including political purges, show trials, and the cult of personality (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the economic and social impact of Stalin's policies, including collectivization, the Five-Year Plans, and the Great Terror.

nodes.name: 毛泽东时代的中国
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the methods used by Mao Zedong to consolidate power in China, including ideological campaigns and mass mobilization (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the social and economic impact of Mao's domestic policies, such as the Great Leap Forward and the Cultural Revolution.

nodes.name: 卡斯特罗的古巴
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the causes of the Cuban Revolution and evaluate the methods used by Fidel Castro to establish and maintain power (HL/SL).
Students will be able to assess the impact of Castro's domestic policies and Cuba's relationship with the superpowers during the Cold War.

nodes.name: 一手史料
curriculum_points: Students will be able to extract and record relevant information from a variety of primary sources, demonstrating comprehension of their explicit meaning (HL/SL).
Students will be able to identify the origin, purpose, and content of primary sources as part of source analysis.

nodes.name: 史料评估（OPVL）
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate the value and limitations of historical sources, with reference to their origin, purpose, and content (HL/SL, Paper 1 skill).
Students will be able to analyse the tone, perspective, and argument presented in a source, placing it in its historical context.

nodes.name: 史学观点比较
curriculum_points: Students will be able to compare and contrast the arguments, perspectives, and methodologies of different historians on a specific historical issue (HL/SL).
Students will be able to explain how and why historical interpretations differ over time.

nodes.name: 历史争鸣
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct a sustained argument that engages with historical debate, using evidence to support their analysis (HL/SL, Paper 2/3 skill).
Students will be able to evaluate the relative merits of different historical interpretations in forming their own reasoned conclusion.

domains.name: IA 与 Paper / IA & Papers / IA & Papers

nodes.name: IA 历史探究
curriculum_points: Students will be able to formulate a clear, focused research question for an historical investigation on a topic of their choice (HL/SL Internal Assessment).
Students will be able to critically analyse a range of sources and historical perspectives in their investigation, leading to a substantiated conclusion.

nodes.name: Paper 1 · 史料题
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse and evaluate primary sources in response to structured questions, demonstrating understanding of origin, purpose, value, and limitation (HL/SL Exam).
Students will be able to synthesize information from multiple sources to construct a coherent short-term response.

nodes.name: Paper 2 · 专题论文
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct two well-structured, analytical essays in response to prompts from two different world history topics (HL/SL Exam).
Students will be able to integrate specific, relevant historical evidence and explicit historiography to support a balanced argument.

nodes.name: Paper 3 · 区域专题（HL）
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct three in-depth, analytical essays on aspects of the history of a specific region, demonstrating detailed knowledge and conceptual understanding (HL Exam).
Students will be able to evaluate different historical perspectives and interpretations in their essays, showing awareness of historiographical debate.
